analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet warns that COVID-19 has accelerated threats to democracy and human rights and asserts that the EU is ready to defend these values globally in the post-pandemic rebuild, addressed to the UN Human Rights Council. Evaluation: EU1 (EU successful story) – not supported; no reference to EU’s historical successes or learning from past. EU2 (EU is united) – not addressed; no mention of internal EU unity or agreements. EU3 (useful for its citizens, within EU) – not applicable; focus is global, not internal citizen benefits. EU4 (avant-garde political actor) – not explicit; while proactive stance is implied, there’s no claim of pioneering regulation or leadership in specific domains like climate/digital. EU5 (EU as global champion of fair causes) – supported; it positions the EU as defending democracy and human rights worldwide. EU6 (EU contributes to a better world) – supported; it frames the EU as contributing to rebuilding the post-pandemic world and engaging multilaterally (message to UN HRC). Conclusion: EU5 and EU6 apply.
